Simon Sangla

Lead Analyst (TripAdvisor) · 16 years building Excel, Sheets & Slides that businesses decide from

Sixteen years building the spreadsheets and decks businesses run on, most recently as Lead Analyst at TripAdvisor. The samples below are real, reproducible work: a workbook and an executive deck built from public economic data, plus a worked example of how I evaluate a document's quality.

Real data · Excel / Google Sheets

Commodity Shock & Inflation Workbook

A reproducible workbook built from free public data (FRED and the World Bank): the 2022 energy and food price shock, indexed and charted, alongside the inflation that followed. It reads as a timeline and says plainly that co-movement is not proof of cause.

Real public dataIndexed time-series chartsReproducible from sourceHonest, caveated framing
Index = price[month] / price[Jan 2022] × 100
Source: FRED (monthly prices) + World Bank (annual inflation)
Download Commodity_Shock_and_Inflation.xlsx
Real data · PowerPoint / Google Slides

Commodity Shock Executive Deck

The same dataset retold for an executive audience: eight PowerPoint slides with native, editable charts (not images) that size the price shock, show how unevenly inflation landed, and stay honest about what the numbers claim. Converts cleanly to Google Slides.

Editable native chartsExecutive narrativeReal public dataPowerPoint + Google Slides
Download Commodity_Shock_Executive_Deck.pptx
Document evaluation

Spreadsheet Evaluation Pack

A rubric-based comparison of two candidate dashboards. Explicit 1–5 criteria, a formula-driven scorecard, and a written rationale where every score points to an observable cell, so a second rater can reproduce it.

Rubric-based evaluationReproducible scoringStructured comparisonWritten rationale
Download Spreadsheet_Evaluation_Pack.xlsx

Simon Sangla · simonsangla@gmail.com

Built reproducibly with Python (openpyxl, python-pptx); screenshots are rendered from the actual files. The commodity workbook and deck are built from real public data (FRED and the World Bank), vendored as a reproducible snapshot; the evaluation pack uses small illustrative figures to demonstrate the rubric.
